title: Israel Keeps Killing Key Iranian Leaders. Will It Work?
summary: Israeli officials trumpeted airstrikes that killed Iran’s de facto leader and the commanders of a militia notorious for violently suppressing protests. But “decapitation has its limits,” an analyst warns.

title: In His Resignation Letter, Joe Kent Spoke About the Death of His Wife
summary: Chief Petty Officer Shannon Kent was killed in action during a special operations mission in Syria in 2019. Mr. Kent said he could not support “sending the next generation off to fight and die” in Iran.

title: Ali Larijani, a Top Iranian Politician and Emissary, Is Dead at 67
summary: Mr. Larijani, the top national security official and a confidant of the former supreme leader, expertly navigated Iran’s internal politics and led the brutal crackdown on protests this year.

title: Jesse Jackson Jr. Seeks Comeback After Corruption Scandal
summary: After his promising career was halted by a corruption scandal that sent him to prison, the former Democratic congressman is trying to return to the House.

title: Year After Trump Takeover, Institute of Peace Is Little More Than Scenery
summary: As a lawsuit by the former staff drags on, the fight highlights President Trump’s upending of traditional peace-building and the lasting effects of his administration’s cost-cutting blitz.

title: No Trump Endorsement for Cornyn or Paxton in Texas Senate Race as Deadline Passes
summary: President Trump’s decision not to weigh in before the deadline means both John Cornyn and Ken Paxton remain on the ballot, extending their costly and increasingly personal battle into a May runoff.

title: Trump Says Newsom Shouldn’t Be President Because He Is Dyslexic
summary: President Trump said “a president should not have learning disabilities,” prompting criticism from a group that advocates equal opportunities for people with learning disabilities.

title: Ruling on RFK Jr.’s Vaccine Policy Changes Leaves Health Groups Scrambling
summary: Lawyers for both sides in the federal lawsuit, brought by six medical organizations, are trying to understand the ramifications of the judge’s decision.

title: Can the Keto Diet Really Improve Mental Health?
summary: Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. recently said that the diet could “cure” schizophrenia. We asked experts about the claim.

title: John Lithgow’s Career Spans 200 Roles — From ‘3rd Rock’ to Roald Dahl
summary: Is there anyone John Lithgow can’t — or won’t — play?

title: Peter Thiel Fears the Antichrist Is Coming. In Rome, Some Call His View Heresy.
summary: The right-wing tech investor is giving lectures near the Catholic church’s administrative heart. Commentators there are rejecting his apocalyptic vision.

title: Why Did Trump Officials Award $2 Million to a Small Art School in Queens?
summary: The National Endowment for the Humanities seldom gave seven-figure grants. Now big awards flow to handpicked projects, including an institution with three full-time employees.

title: Judge Ejects Federal Prosecutor From Court and Orders Bosses to Testify
summary: Judge Zahid Quraishi ordered a hearing on who had the authority to lead New Jersey’s top federal law enforcement office.

title: How Readers Voted on Miscast Roles in the Movies
summary: Given a list of 14 mismatches, they quickly picked Ben Platt in “Dear Evan Hansen.” But they took issue (loudly) with one of our choices.

title: Oscars Viewership Slides 9%, Its First Drop Since 2021
summary: The 17.9 million total followed similar drops for the Grammys and the Golden Globes.

title: Academy ‘Extremely Upset’ After Teyana Taylor Says Oscars Security Shoved Her
summary: The academy said an employee of an outside security firm hired for the Oscars had “incidental contact” with the actress, which it called “not acceptable.”

title: 25 Recipes and 3 Menus for the Ultimate Gathering
summary: Make one or make all: Yewande Komolafe’s curated menus are meant for celebrating.

title: For Once, We Fight With an Equal Ally
summary: Israel is the rare U.S. ally that pulls its weight, shares the risk and contributes to victory.

title: The Unlikely Alliance to Save East Palestine, Ohio
summary: In Ohio after a toxic chemical accident, finding common ground in the country’s health wars.

title: Iran’s Leadership Suffers a Double Blow
summary: Also, the Senate begins a bitter debate about voting rules. Here’s the latest at the end of Tuesday.

title: An Off-Duty Officer Shot a Man in the Head, N.Y.P.D. Says
summary: The officer, whom police did not identify but who works at Gracie Mansion and City Hall, is now under investigation. The man who was shot, a 30-year-old, is in critical condition.

title: Miami Cubans Fear Trump Won’t Go Far Enough: ‘The Castros Have to Go’
summary: Many Cubans in Florida have sought regime change for decades. They fear that President Trump’s talks with Cuba will not lead to wholesale political transformation.

title: Plus-Size Southwest Passengers Say New Extra-Seat Policy Has Led to Fat Shaming
summary: Since the airline changed its policy on larger passengers this year, travelers say agents have publicly scrutinized their bodies and made them buy extra seats.
